title: Prolactin result review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports recording serum prolactin result context, repeat or macroprolactin context when clinician-selected and symptoms relevant to an elevated result.
investigations.rationale: The exact diagnosis section supports result source and date, symptom and negative context and clinician-reviewed prolactin laboratory documentation without asserting hyperprolactinaemia.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording serum prolactin result context, repeat or macroprolactin context when clinician-selected and symptoms relevant to an elevated result.

source.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Hyperprolactinemia: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

investigations.text: Supports clinician review of medicine, renal, thyroid, physiological and pituitary context and already performed imaging without assigning a cause.
investigations.rationale: The exact cause-evaluation section supports focused endocrine assessment and previous specialist correspondence without assigning a medicine, renal, thyroid or pituitary cause.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician review of medicine, renal, thyroid, physiological and pituitary context and already performed imaging without assigning a cause.

source.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Hyperprolactinemia: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 2.1–3.3 — cause, medicine and imaging evaluation

investigations.text: Supports clinician-led symptom, laboratory, imaging, adverse-effect, pregnancy and follow-up review. Medicine names, doses and treatment instructions are excluded.
investigations.rationale: The exact follow-up section supports patient concerns, clinician-entered plan, discussed safety net, arranged follow-up and questions without creating treatment instructions.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led symptom, laboratory, imaging, adverse-effect, pregnancy and follow-up review. Medicine names, doses and treatment instructions are excluded.

source.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Hyperprolactinemia: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline

sources_used.title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Hyperprolactinemia: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline
sources_used.jurisdiction: International specialty guideline; UAE laboratory, prescribing, imaging and referral policy governs local use

source_limitations: Value, units, reference range, sampling conditions, pregnancy, medicines, renal and thyroid function, symptoms, imaging, interpretation, diagnosis and treatment remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
